refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall minimize cycle time for each phase of the load→travel→dump cycle to meet the customer-defined cycle time target.
refined_fr.rationale: Decomposing the overall cycle time KPI into sub-FRs for each phase allows targeted design parameters and implementation modules to optimize each phase, improving clarity and traceability.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure p95 cycle time for load, travel, and dump phases individually under agreed duty cycle and site conditions, then aggregate to verify overall cycle time target.
sub_frs: FR1: Minimize load phase cycle time
FR2: Minimize travel phase cycle time
FR3: Minimize dump phase cycle time

candidate_dps: DP1: Optimize vehicle mechanical and control systems for load phase efficiency
DP2: Optimize vehicle navigation and path planning for travel phase efficiency
DP3: Optimize vehicle dumping mechanism and sequencing for dump phase efficiency
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1, DP2, DP3 combined to address each sub-FR distinctly

potential_sources: Interdependencies between vehicle control during load and travel phases
Coordination between dumping mechanism and travel completion
coupling_check.notes: DPs are logically decoupled by phase but require interface coordination; manageable coupling with clear interface definitions.

assumptions_risks: Assumes vehicle subsystems can be optimized independently without violating safety constraints
Risk of suboptimal integration if phase optimizations conflict
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Monitor integration during implementation to ensure phase optimizations do not degrade overall cycle time or safety.

im_boundary.name: Vehicle
im_boundary.scope: Physical and control systems responsible for load, travel, and dump phases

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Vehicle sends cycle phase status and performance data to NGHE Solution

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Vehicle receives operational commands and cycle phase instructions from NGHE Solution

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Vehicle sends safety alerts to Safety Services

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Communication channel for remote control and monitoring via Teleoperation and NGHE Solution

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment of cycle time related functions; low diffusion as core physical and control systems are centralized here.
Contains supervisory control and analytics but cycle time optimization logic is mostly in Vehicle.
Limited direct role in cycle time optimization; mainly remote control interface.
Focus on safety alerts, minimal direct cycle time function.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing cycle time optimization in Vehicle module minimizes diffusion and improves control over physical and control parameters.
containment_risks: Potential complexity in Vehicle module due to integrating multiple phase optimizations
Risk of insufficient coordination with NGHE Solution supervisory functions
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define clear interface contracts and modularize Vehicle control software to manage complexity.

fr_dp_gate.reason: FRs are well decomposed into distinct phases with corresponding DPs that logically address each sub-FR with manageable coupling.

diffusion_risks: Integration complexity within Vehicle module due to multiple phase optimizations
Potential interface mismatches with NGHE Solution supervisory layer
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Vehicle module shows strong containment of cycle time functions, minimizing diffusion risk.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by measuring phase cycle times via Telemetry interface and validating against customer targets.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of cycle time optimization to Vehicle module with well-defined external interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Modularize Vehicle control software to separate load, travel, and dump phase logic
Establish rigorous interface definitions between Vehicle and NGHE Solution
Plan integration testing to address coupling risks

decision.justification: Current decomposition and assignments meet criteria with clear FR->DP->IM mapping, manageable coupling, and feasible verification. No immediate need to zigzag or revise at this level.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Minimize load phase cycle time
FR2: Minimize travel phase cycle time
FR3: Minimize dump phase cycle time

revise.why: No significant issues detected requiring revision at this level.

assumptions: Vehicle subsystems can be optimized independently
Interfaces between Vehicle and NGHE Solution will be clearly defined and stable
evidence_needed: Performance data from phase-specific cycle time measurements
Interface compliance test results between Vehicle and NGHE Solution
